name: Apple Butter Stack Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream butter and sugar for at least 10 minutes by hand, or 5 minutes in an electric mixer until the mixture is thick and white.
2. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Stir in vanilla.
4. If the baker's ammonia is in granular form, crush to a powder with a rolling pin or bottle.
5. Sift flour and baker's ammonia together.
6. Stir flour into egg mixture and blend thoroughly. The dough will be soft.
7. Chill for at least 30 minutes.
8. Cut 6 to 8 - 8-inch rounds from waxed paper.
9. Spread the dough thinly and evenly on paper rounds, leaving about a half-inch margin for expansion during baking.
10. Place two or three round (whatever it will hold) on a baking sheet and bake at 400°F for 8 to 10 minutes until golden brown on edges. Remove immediately from the cookie sheet but do not remove paper until ready to assemble the cake.
11. Assemble by placing well-spiced apple butter between each layer. Leave top unadorned.
The baker's ammonia (ammonium carbonate or hartshorn) may be purchased at drugstores. Or 3 3/4 teaspoons baking powder may be substituted.
